story.asA: photographer visiting Recipe for the first time
story.iWant: a clear, professional landing page explaining what Recipe is and how to use it
story.soThat: I can quickly understand the tool's purpose, see the privacy promise, and start converting presets within 60 seconds

task.title: Design Landing Page Content Structure
task.subtask: Define page structure with 7 sections: hero, description, how-to-use, privacy, formats, FAQ, footer
Write content for each section targeting non-technical photographers
Review content for jargon removal and clarity

task.title: Implement Landing Page in HTML
task.subtask: Update web/index.html with new landing sections
Preserve existing converter UI from Epic 2
Add semantic HTML structure and navigation links

task.title: Style Landing Page with CSS
task.subtask: Add styles for landing sections in web/static/style.css
Ensure mobile-responsive design (375px, 768px, 1920px breakpoints)
Maintain consistency with existing Epic 2 styles

task.title: Create Format Compatibility Matrix
task.subtask: Create docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md with parameter mapping table
Document all 3 formats (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate) and bidirectional conversion paths
Link from landing page to detailed matrix

task.title: Create FAQ Documentation
task.subtask: Create docs/faq.md covering legal, privacy, technical questions
Embed top 4 questions on landing page with link to full FAQ

task.title: Update README.md
task.subtask: Add deployment URL section pointing to recipe.pages.dev
Add browser support section listing Chrome/Firefox/Safari
Update "For Developers" section with technical links

task.title: Test Landing Page for Non-Technical Users
task.subtask: Recruit 1-2 non-technical users for comprehension test
Allow 60 seconds reading time, ask comprehension questions
Iterate on content if user fails to explain Recipe correctly

task.title: Validate All Links
task.subtask: Manually click all documentation and technical links
Verify destinations correct and no 404 errors

task.title: Deploy Landing Page
task.subtask: Commit changes to git (index.html, style.css, docs)
Push to main branch triggering Cloudflare deployment
Verify site live and all features working

ac.title: Project Description Section
ac.criteria: Landing page contains clear "What is Recipe?" section
Describes Recipe as universal photo preset converter for NP3/XMP/lrtemplate formats
Written in non-technical language targeting Nikon Z photographers
Maximum 150 words, scannable structure with headline and 2-3 paragraphs
ac.verification: Manual review for clarity, comprehension test with non-technical user

ac.title: Three-Step Usage Guide
ac.criteria: Exactly 3 numbered steps documented: Upload, Convert, Download
Each step has concise description (one sentence)
Visual clarity with numbered list or icons
Visible on landing page without scrolling
ac.verification: Manual review of step count and placement

ac.title: Privacy Promise Prominently Displayed
ac.criteria: Privacy statement headline: "100% Client-Side Processing" or "Your Files Never Leave Your Device"
Explains WebAssembly processing, no server uploads, zero tracking
Visually distinct section (border, background, icon)
Positioned early on page (users see within 3 seconds)
Links to browser compatibility privacy validation
ac.verification: Manual review of visibility and messaging clarity

ac.title: Format Compatibility Matrix Accessible
ac.criteria: Compatibility matrix linked or embedded on landing page
Shows 3 formats with bidirectional conversion support
Mentions 95%+ accuracy for core parameters
Link to detailed matrix in docs/ directory
ac.verification: Click link, verify matrix loads and displays correctly

ac.title: FAQ Section Linked or Embedded
ac.criteria: FAQ section exists with minimum 4 questions: legal, privacy, features, accuracy
Answers are 2-4 sentences each, concise and clear
Link to complete FAQ documentation
ac.verification: Read answers for clarity, verify link works

ac.title: Readable by Non-Technical Users
ac.criteria: No technical jargon without explanation
Clear headings and scannable structure
Mobile-responsive layout (tested at 375px, 768px, 1920px)
Minimum 16px font size, touch-friendly buttons (44px height)
ac.verification: User comprehension test: non-technical user can explain Recipe after 60 seconds

ac.title: Links to Technical Documentation
ac.criteria: Footer contains "For Developers" section with technical links
Links to GitHub repository, architecture docs, format specifications
All links working and point to correct documentation
ac.verification: Click each link, verify destination loads

docs.doc: Recipe is a privacy-first, browser-based photo preset converter for Nikon Z photographers to use Lightroom presets on their cameras. Key value proposition: 95%+ color accuracy, 100% client-side processing via WebAssembly, first-of-its-kind bidirectional NP3↔XMP↔lrtemplate conversion. Target audience: Nikon Z-series photographers, especially Z f users wanting access to vast Lightroom preset library.

7 acceptance criteria for landing page: project description, 3-step usage guide, privacy promise, format compatibility matrix, FAQ, non-technical readability, technical documentation links. Content must be scannable, jargon-free, and enable users to start converting within 60 seconds. Privacy messaging critical to differentiate from server-based competitors.

Recipe deploys to Cloudflare Pages (static hosting, global CDN, auto HTTPS). Web interface consists of vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with WASM binary for client-side conversion. Zero server infrastructure, zero tracking. Browser compatibility: Chrome 131+, Firefox 132+, Safari 18.0+, Edge 131+ (90%+ market coverage).

Privacy by design is core differentiator. Files processed entirely in-browser via WebAssembly. No installation, no server uploads, no waiting. "The Magic Moment": photographer drags Lightroom preset into Recipe, clicks Convert, downloads NP3 file in seconds, loads on camera - 95%+ color accuracy. Pure privacy, instant results.

Landing page must load in <2 seconds first visit, <500ms cached. HTML size <50KB, total page size <200KB. Time to First Contentful Paint <1.5 seconds. Cloudflare CDN latency <100ms globally. No JavaScript frameworks for documentation sections (vanilla HTML/CSS only).

Epic 2 delivered functional web converter with drag-drop upload, format detection, parameter preview, target format selection, WASM conversion, file download, error handling, privacy messaging, and responsive design. All stories (2-1 through 2-10) complete and production-ready. Story 7-1 adds documentation layers to existing converter UI.

artifact.reason: Existing web interface structure to be enhanced with landing page sections

code.artifact: Contains header with hero section, privacy badge, file upload drop zone, parameter display, conversion controls, download button, and footer with privacy FAQ. Story 7-1 adds: project description section, 3-step usage guide, format compatibility table, technical documentation links in footer.

artifact.reason: Existing styles for web interface to be extended with landing page styles

code.artifact: Contains styles for header/hero, drop zone, status banners, error messages, parameter panels, conversion controls, privacy badge, privacy FAQ, footer. Story 7-1 adds: styles for new landing sections, responsive breakpoints for mobile (375px, 768px), typography for documentation content.

artifact.reason: Repository documentation to be updated with deployment URLs and browser support

code.artifact: Current content: building CLI, usage examples, development instructions. Story 7-1 adds: web interface section with recipe.pages.dev URL, browser support section (Chrome/Firefox/Safari), privacy guarantee messaging, "For Developers" section with architecture/format spec links.

package.name: vanilla-html-css-js
package.usage: No framework dependencies - static HTML/CSS/JS for documentation

package.name: cloudflare-pages
package.usage: Static site hosting and global CDN

constraints.constraint: No technical jargon without explanation - target audience is photographers, not developers. Use "runs in your browser" instead of "WebAssembly", "color grading" instead of "parameter mapping"
Privacy-first messaging mandatory - prominently display "Your files never leave your device" promise. Link to browser compatibility validation as proof.
Landing page load time <2 seconds (first visit), <500ms (cached). HTML <50KB, total page <200KB. No JavaScript frameworks for documentation content.
Mobile-responsive required - test at 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1920px (desktop). Minimum 16px font, 44px touch targets. Maintain Epic 2 design consistency.
Scannable structure with clear headings (H1→H2→H3). Non-technical users must understand Recipe purpose within 60 seconds. WCAG AAA compliance inherited from Epic 2 Story 2-10.
Preserve all Epic 2 converter functionality - landing sections prepend existing upload/conversion UI. No breaking changes to web/index.html structure or CSS classes.
Include reverse engineering disclosure and no-warranty statement (Story 7-4 Legal Disclaimer). Recommend private use until legal assessment complete.

interface.name: Cloudflare Pages Deployment
interface.signature: Push to main branch → GitHub Actions builds WASM → Deploys web/ directory to Cloudflare Pages → Live at https://recipe.pages.dev

interface.name: Web Navigation
interface.signature: Landing page internal links: #privacyFAQ (privacy section), #formats (compatibility), #developers (footer tech links). External links: GitHub repository, architecture docs, browser compatibility.

interface.name: Documentation Structure
interface.signature: docs/format-compatibility-matrix.md - Parameter mapping table for NP3/XMP/lrtemplate. docs/faq.md - Legal, privacy, technical Q&A. README.md - Installation and deployment URLs.

tests.standards: Manual testing approach for documentation quality and user comprehension. No automated tests for static content. Testing standards: (1) Content review by SM for clarity, accuracy, jargon removal. (2) Non-technical user comprehension test - 1-2 photographers read landing page for 60 seconds, then answer: "What does Recipe do?", "How do you use it?", "Is your data private?". Pass criteria: user can explain in their own words. (3) Link validation - manually click all links, verify destinations load, no 404 errors. (4) Responsive design test - open in browser at 1920px, 768px, 375px viewports, verify layout adapts, text readable, buttons accessible. (5) Lighthouse audit - performance >90, accessibility 100 (WCAG AAA), best practices >90. (6) Deployment verification - visit recipe.pages.dev, confirm landing page live, all sections render correctly.

locations.location: Manual testing - no test files required
User comprehension test notes in story completion record

ideas.idea: Content Clarity Test - Non-technical user reads "What is Recipe?" section, explains it back. Success: mentions preset conversion, Nikon cameras, Lightroom presets, no server uploads.
Usage Guide Completeness - Count steps in "How to Use" section. Success: exactly 3 steps (Upload, Convert, Download), each with one-sentence description.
Privacy Visibility Test - Fresh user loads landing page, time how long to find privacy promise. Success: user notices "Your files never leave your device" within 3 seconds.
Matrix Accessibility Test - User looks for format support info, clicks compatibility matrix link. Success: finds link within 10 seconds, matrix loads showing 3 formats.
FAQ Findability Test - User has question "Is this legal?", searches FAQ. Success: finds answer in <30 seconds, answer is 2-4 sentences, cites fair use.
Mobile Responsive Test - Open landing page on phone (375px), verify readable text, no horizontal scroll, buttons tappable. Success: all content accessible, no zoom required.
Developer Link Test - Advanced user looks for technical docs, clicks GitHub link in footer. Success: link works, opens repository in new tab.
Lighthouse Performance Audit - Run lighthouse audit on deployed landing page. Target: Performance >90, Accessibility 100, Best Practices >90, SEO >80.
Browser Compatibility Spot Check - Open recipe.pages.dev in Chrome, Firefox, Safari. Verify landing page renders identically, no layout issues.
Content Length Validation - Count words in "What is Recipe?" section. Success: 100-150 words (scannable, not overwhelming).
